description: Two-phase settlement. Antecedent is the immediately preceding message in the settlement chain. Phases: buyer-accept/buyer-reject (sent by buyer after receiving match); put-accept/put-reject (sent by exchange after receiving put); deliver (exchange sends content reference to buyer); complete (buyer confirms receipt and hash); dispute (buyer contests content — triggers escrow hold). Sender roles: buyer sends buyer-accept, buyer-reject, complete, dispute; exchange operator sends put-accept, put-reject, deliver.

args.name: phase
args.description: Settlement phase. Composed into exchange:phase:<value> tag.

args.name: accepted
args.description: Buyer's accept/reject decision. Used in buyer-accept and buyer-reject phases.

args.name: reason
args.description: Reason for rejection or dispute. Optional.

args.name: price
args.description: Agreed price in scrip (micro-tokens). Required in put-accept and complete phases.

args.name: content_hash_verified
args.description: Whether content hash was verified against delivered content. Required in complete phase.

args.name: dispute_type
args.description: Type of dispute. Required in dispute phase.

args.name: verdict
args.description: Settlement verdict. Composed into exchange:verdict:<value> tag. Omitted for intermediate phases (deliver, put-accept, put-reject).
